description: SeedVR2 diffusion VIDEO UPSCALER (numz/ComfyUI-SeedVR2_VideoUpscaler, model by ByteDance-Seed / IceClear). One-step diffusion super-resolution that restores real detail (not just a bigger blur) while keeping motion temporally stable. Graph: the upstream 'SeedVR2_HD_video_upscale' example, cleaned by seedvr2.derive.py into a flat LoadVideo -> GetVideoComponents -> SeedVR2VideoUpscaler -> CreateVideo -> SaveVideo (the bypassed RGBA-join + torch-compile convenience nodes are stripped; comfy_run's converter has no bypass-passthrough). Ready-to-run: the 7B weights (~15 GB) + VAE are pinned/public. Built for BATCH — feed many clips and/or image-sequence folders in one job with --batch.

about.what: Upscales video to a higher resolution and rebuilds genuine detail — faces, textures, edges — with a one-step diffusion model, instead of just enlarging (and softening) the frames. Keeps motion steady frame-to-frame.
about.inputs: One or more video clips and/or folders of image frames (EXR / PNG / JPG / TIFF). With --batch you can mix many of both in a single job — they all upscale on one warm GPU, each output named after its input.
about.key_knobs: Resolution — target size of the SHORT edge (1080 = upscale to 1080p tall). Model — 7B (sharpest, default) or the lighter 3B / 7B-fp8. Batch size — frames processed together (must be 1, 5, 9, 13… = 4n+1); higher = steadier motion + more VRAM.

_HOWITRUNS: No-build path (same as the other presets): the public yanwk image runs comfy_run.py, which clones the SeedVR2 node pack (+ VideoHelperSuite when PNG/JPG/TIFF sequences are batched, CoCoTools for EXR), downloads the 7B DiT + VAE (~15.8 GB) into models/SEEDVR2 on the node, converts this flat UI graph to API via /object_info, applies patches, and renders. --convert-only skips the weight pull and validates the graph cheaply. BATCH: with --batch the runner loops every input through this one warm graph — the model loads ONCE — re-pointing the loader per item (a video filename, or an EXR/PNG sequence loader swapped in at GetVideoComponents) and naming each output after its input.
_BATCH: EXR folders are read scene-linear→sRGB; PNG/JPG/TIFF folders load as-is (display). A folder is auto-detected as a numbered frame sequence. DPX is not supported yet. Picking a non-default model (3B or 7B-fp8) in the form/CLI downloads it on first use (only 7B-fp16 is pre-pinned). For tighter VRAM, use the 7B-fp8 or 3B model, a smaller GPU, or set blocks_to_swap on the DiT loader.
_PIPNOTE: The node's requirements (diffusers>=0.33.1, unpinned transformers) resolve to a combo on the cu130 image where 'diffusers.loaders.single_file_model' import dies with KeyError: 'flash_attn' (a transformers/diffusers flash-attn API mismatch; node import fails, SeedVR2VideoUpscaler never registers, convert gate fails). pip_extra pins a known-good pair AFTER the node reqs. If it still fails, try installing flash-attn or bumping these pins.

primary_input.label: Input video (or use Batch for many)
primary_input.tooltip: The clip to upscale. Output resolution follows the 'Resolution' knob (target short edge); the aspect ratio of the input is preserved. For a whole shot folder of frames, or many clips at once, use Batch mode.

batch_input.label: Batch inputs (videos and/or image-sequence folders)
batch_input.tooltip: Add any number of video files and/or folders of frames (EXR / PNG / JPG / TIFF). They all upscale in ONE job on a single warm GPU — far cheaper than one job each — and each output is named after its input.

ui.label: Resolution (short edge)
ui.tooltip: Target size of the SHORTER edge, in pixels; the aspect ratio is kept (1080 on a 16:9 clip → 1920×1080). This is the upscale target — set it higher than your source's short edge. (src: SeedVR2 Video Upscaler 'resolution')

ui.label: Model
ui.tooltip: 7B-fp16 = best detail (the default, pre-loaded ~15 GB, RTX PRO 6000). 7B-fp8 ≈ half the VRAM, near-7B quality. 3B-fp16 = fastest / fits smaller GPUs. Non-default picks download on first use. (src: SeedVR2 model registry)

ui.label: Batch size (frames per pass)
ui.tooltip: Frames processed together. MUST be 1, 5, 9, 13, 17… (4n+1). Higher = steadier motion across the clip and faster, but more VRAM; lower = less VRAM. Ideally near your shot length. (src: SeedVR2 'batch_size')

ui.label: Color correction
ui.tooltip: Re-matches the upscaled colours to the source (upscaling can shift them). 'lab' is the recommended default; 'none' disables it. (src: SeedVR2 'color_correction')

ui.label: Seed
ui.tooltip: Random seed — same seed + same inputs = identical output. (src: SeedVR2 'seed')

ui.label: Max resolution (0 = no cap)
ui.tooltip: Caps the LONG edge so extreme aspect ratios don't blow up VRAM. 0 = no limit; if any edge would exceed this, both are scaled down together. (src: SeedVR2 'max_resolution')

ui.label: Temporal overlap
ui.tooltip: Frames shared between consecutive batches, blended to smooth the seam at batch boundaries. 1–4 helps on long clips; 0 = off. (src: SeedVR2 'temporal_overlap')
